outlook.headline: CPU demand is broadening from AI novelty to infrastructure necessity
outlook.summary: CPU commentary turned sharply more positive this quarter, with the mention trend accelerating to 2.6x and broad-based reads from AI, cloud, and server names. The strongest demand is coming from agentic/inference workloads and rising datacenter CPU content, while tightness at FormFactor and stronger build signals at AMD, Intel, and Amkor suggest demand is outrunning near-term supply in parts of the chain. Watch whether this remains a real order-driven step-up versus just incremental AI-stack exposure, and whether the current enthusiasm extends beyond a few lead customers.

drivers.label: AI/agentic workloads lifting CPU relevance
drivers.detail: Multiple companies said agentic AI and inference are adding incremental CPU demand, making CPUs more central to AI infrastructure.

drivers.label: Datacenter and cloud CPU ramps broadening
drivers.detail: Managements described new server CPU SKUs, resurgent demand, and more CPU demand from major cloud and enterprise customers.

drivers.label: Higher content per socket / attach opportunities
drivers.detail: AI-era CPUs are pulling in more memory and adjacent content, supporting demand for memory, interconnect, and attach suppliers.

drivers.label: Supply tightness in parts of the ecosystem
drivers.detail: Some CPU-related test/capacity commentary points to demand running ahead of supply, reinforcing a tighter near-term setup.

drivers.label: Mentions are accelerating across the sector
drivers.detail: Company mention frequency rose to 2.6x with strong momentum versus the trailing average, signaling broader investor and management focus on CPU demand.

risks: If AI/server build plans slip, the current CPU demand surge could prove timing-driven rather than durable.
A few comments are still qualitative or adjacent to CPU rather than direct standalone demand confirmation.
Broadening must hold beyond a handful of hyperscalers and lead customers to sustain the upgrade cycle.
